highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 adds FreeType-backed font support on Windows Nano Server and ships targeted performance improvements.
highlights_body: A long-standing gap — fonts on Nano Server — is closed with a community contribution enabling FreeType as the font backend. Alongside that, two hot-path allocations are eliminated and a memory leak in the raster image failure path is fixed.

bullets.lead: Font rendering on Windows Nano Server
bullets.detail: Nano Server ships without the Windows GDI/DirectWrite font stack. SkiaSharp now uses FreeType as the font backend on that target so text rendering works out of the box.

CI now validates SkiaSharp on Azure Linux, Alpine (musl libc), and Nano Server via dedicated container test legs, ensuring the library works correctly across the full range of supported Linux and Windows Server targets.

bullets.lead: Memory leak in SKImage raster creation fixed
bullets.detail: When the native raster image creation failed, the CoTaskMem pixel buffer allocated for the operation was not freed, causing a native memory leak. The failure path now correctly releases the buffer.

GetEncodedText no longer allocates an intermediate string on the null-terminated fast path, and SKRuntimeEffectUniforms now pre-sizes its internal lookup map to the known uniform count — both removing unnecessary allocations on frequently called code paths.

contributor_summaries.jeffska: Added FreeType-backed font rendering for Windows Nano Server, closing a long-standing gap for containerized workloads
